title: Middle-passive lexicon review: no direct source-cache support
scope: Highest-volume lexicon-only middle-passive action groups with zero direct Husic/Kaikki source-cache support. Decisions here use UniParser lexeme/analyzer evidence plus retained local corpus distribution; they are not exact paradigm-source attestations.

decisionMeaning.keep: Retain generated middle-passive forms; lexeme/analyzer evidence and retained corpus distribution support them enough for now.
decisionMeaning.noMiddlePassive: Suppress generated middle-passive forms after source review confirms non-active forms should not be produced.
decisionMeaning.middlePassiveThirdPersonOnly: Restrict finite middle-passive output to third person after evidence supports real third-person passive use but not personal first/second-person generation.
decisionMeaning.needs_source: Do not change generation yet; current local evidence is insufficient for a generation decision.

rows.reviewNote: UniParser has an explicit medial/transitive lexeme and retained middle-passive hits exist, but support is sparse and mostly third-person; this argues against pruning but does not prove the full analytic paradigm.

decisionEvidence.basis: UniParser analyzer accepts generated middle-passive head forms for kooperoj, and retained examples include non-active forms such as kooperohem, kooperohet, and kooperohen. This is morphology support, not frequency evidence.

rows.reviewNote: The lexeme is transitive/intransitive and the analyzer supports head components, but there is no medial lexeme tag or direct source-cache paradigm for the full phrase bucket.

decisionEvidence.basis: UniParser analyzer accepts farmakos middle-passive forms and retained examples include farmakosem, farmakoseni, u farmakosëm, and farmakoset. Some examples are noisy, but the non-active form itself is locally supported.

Retained middle-passive evidence is overwhelmingly third person: strong examples include firmohet, firmohen, u firmua, and është firmuar; the few non-third hits are malformed or corrupted diacriticless text.
Analyzer acceptance alone is weaker than the retained corpus distribution: credible passive evidence is third-person, while non-third examples such as firmohesh are malformed.

rows.reviewNote: The lexeme is transitive/intransitive and local examples include non-active forms, but the large missing bucket is still only head-component supported and full-phrase unchecked.

decisionEvidence.basis: UniParser analyzer accepts generated middle-passive forms and local retained examples include plausible non-active uses across non-third and third person, such as kuvendohem, kuvendohemi, kuvendohesh, and kuvendohet.

rows.reviewNote: UniParser has medial and intransitive entries, but dhemb/dhëmb ambiguity, nonstandard tags, dental-form false positives, and dative/experiencer syntax make the local evidence too ambiguous for a generation change.
Analyzer accepts a few forms and local examples include dhembem/dhembesha/të dhembesh, but false positives such as dental dhembesh/dhembet need separation before keep or restriction.

decisionEvidence.basis: UniParser lexeme evidence tags farkëtoj as both medial and transitive, the analyzer accepts multiple middle-passive head forms, and retained local corpus examples attest non-third-person and third-person middle-passive usage such as farkëtohem, farkëtohemi, farkëtohesh, jam farkëtuar, and jemi farkëtuar.
Analyzer acceptance aligns with retained non-third-person middle-passive evidence for farkëtoj; this is lexeme-plus-corpus support, not exact Husić source-cache support.

Retained middle-passive evidence is overwhelmingly third person: strong examples include fabrikohet, fabrikohen, u fabrikua, and është fabrikuar; the only non-third surface is a duplicated corrupted diacriticless phrase, not reliable personal passive evidence.

UniParser lexeme evidence tags gudulis as both medial and transitive, and retained local corpus examples attest middle-passive usage across first, second, and third person forms such as gudulisem, gudulisemi, gudulisesh, guduliseni, u gudulisa, u gudulisëm, and janë gudulisur.
Analyzer-supported gudulis middle-passive heads align with retained examples across person and number, supporting keep rather than a third-person-only restriction.
